2605.08019.title: Reason to Play: Behavioral and Brain Alignment Between Frontier LRMs and Human Game Learners
2605.08019.paper_summary: Humans rapidly learn abstract knowledge when encountering novel environments and flexibly deploy this knowledge to guide efficient and intelligent action. Can modern AI systems learn and plan in a similar way? We study this question using a dataset of complex human gameplay with concurrent fMRI recordings, in which participants learn novel video games that require rule discovery, hypothesis revision, and multi-step planning. We jointly evaluate models by their ability to play the games, match human learning behavior, and predict brain activity during the same task, comparing a suite of frontier Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) against model-free and model-based deep reinforcement learning agents and a Bayesian theory-based agent. We find that frontier LRMs most closely match human behavioral patterns during game discovery and predict brain activity an order of magnitude better than both reinforcement learning alternatives across cortical and subcortical regions, with effects robust to permutation controls. Through targeted manipulations, we further show that brain alignment reflects the model's in-context representation of the game state rather than its downstream planning or reasoning. Our results establish LRMs as compelling computational accounts of human learning and decision making in complex, naturalistic environments. Project page with interactive replays: https://botcs.github.io/reason-to-play/
2605.08019.paper_summary_zh: 人類在面對新環境時迅速學習抽象知識，並靈活地運用這些知識來指導高效且智能的行動。現代人工智慧系統能以類似的方式學習和規劃嗎？我們利用一個包含複雜人類遊戲玩法的數據集進行研究，並同時進行fMRI錄影，參與者在其中學習需要規則發現、假設修正和多步規劃的新視頻遊戲。我們通過評估模型在遊戲中的表現、與人類學習行為的匹配程度以及在相同任務中預測大腦活動的能力來進行聯合評估，並將一系列前沿大型推理模型（LRMs）與無模型和基於模型的深度強化學習代理以及基於貝葉斯理論的代理進行比較。我們發現，前沿LRMs在遊戲發現過程中最接近人類行為模式，並在皮層和皮層下區域中預測大腦活動的準確度比兩種強化學習替代方案高出一個數量級，且效果對置換控制具有穩健性。通過針對性的操控，我們進一步顯示大腦對齊反映了模型在上下文中對遊戲狀態的表示，而非其下游的規劃或推理。我們的結果確立了LRMs作為人類在複雜自然環境中學習和決策的引人注目的計算解釋。項目頁面及互動重播：https://botcs.github.io/reason-to-play/
2605.08019.author: Botos Csaba et.al.
2605.08019.authors: Botos Csaba, Sreejan Kumar, Austin Tudor David Andrews, Laurence Hunt, Chris Summerfield, Joshua B. Tenenbaum, Rui Ponte Costa, Marcelo G. Mattar, Momchil Tomov
